text:
Hey, I know this isn't the news you were hoping for, and it's okay to feel disappointed right now. Failing an exam you cared about genuinely stings, and you don't have to pretend otherwise.

But here's what I want you to hold onto: this moment is not the final word on your ability, your intelligence, or your future. Some of the most capable people you'll ever meet have a failure like this somewhere in their story. What separates them isn't that they never fell short — it's that they didn't let falling short become the end of the story.

You now know something you didn't know before. You know where the gaps are, what the pressure feels like, and what you're up against. That's not nothing — that's actually a foundation. The next attempt, you go in with real information instead of assumptions.

Take a little time to breathe and process. Then, when you're ready, look at what happened with clear eyes. Not to beat yourself up, but to understand. Was it preparation? Nerves? The material itself? Time management on the day? Each of those has a solution, and you're smart enough to find it.

You haven't lost anything permanent here. You've hit a delay, and delays are not defeats. The people rooting for you haven't stopped rooting. Come back to this when you're ready, and come back swinging.
